handoff.reason: Option (a) — single analyzer slice — supersedes Option 1. CORE flagged the architecture issue post-Option-1-ruling and before DISPATCH-006 was accepted. Pivoted clean. Plan + analysis + context authored; ready for CODEREF-CORE execution.

execution_rules.phase_gates: Phase 1 (investigation) gates Phase 2 (implementation); Phase 2 gates Phase 3 (tests); Phase 3 gates Phase 4 (close)
execution_rules.atomic_commits: one per task per fix target; single push at end of each phase
execution_rules.tsc_invariant: tsc --noEmit clean both configs (tsconfig.json + tsconfig.cli.json) after each commit

hard_constraints: Phase 7 archive MUST NOT be reopened (rebuild COMPLETE)
Superseded WO directory (rag-index-analyzer-slice-coverage) preserved; only communication.json status block already updated
tsc --noEmit clean both configs after each commit
Cultural validator scope-mode + tracking validator scope-mode must PASS clean

communication_log.summary: WO-RAG-INDEX-SINGLE-ANALYZER-SLICE-001 authored as replacement for superseded WO-RAG-INDEX-ANALYZER-SLICE-COVERAGE-001. Plan, analysis, context, communication all in WO dir. Ready for DISPATCH-007 execution. AC-09 target: identity (chunksSkipped === header_missing_count exactly), not ±10% band. Net negative LOC expected. Stub closes via supersession.

Phase 1 probe (commit 9d84677) revealed AC-05 wording ('chunksSkipped === header_missing_count exactly, 262===262') is mathematically unreachable post-pivot. graph.json ships 2771 element-grain nodes (2410 with headerStatus='missing') covering 262 unique files. Element-grain chunks post-pivot will produce ~2410 skips, not 262. Surfaced three paths (A/B/C); recommended (B) — dual-AC framing with file-grain aggregation in test code. HALT pending ORCHESTRATOR ruling. Phase 1 commit pushed-ready; no implementation until ruling lands.

Ruling: (B) — dual-AC framing. AC-05 splits into AC-05a (element-grain identity ~2410===~2410) + AC-05b (file-grain identity 262===262, computed via uniqueFiles aggregation in test code). Both load-bearing, both strict, no ±10% tolerance. Zero IndexingResult schema delta — chunksSkippedDetails already exists; uniques counted in test assertion. Path 3.3 rewrites the predecessor invariant test (drops ±10% language); fixture must have multiple nodes per file to make AC-05a and AC-05b distinct. Phase 4 dynamic re-verify checks BOTH identities. Plan + analysis + communication updated; CORE resumes from Phase 1 commit 9d84677 with corrected ACs.

Phase 4 dynamic E2E rag-index hung at ~25 min (~9× chunk count due to element-grain pivot caused embedding workload to balloon). The embedding step is irrelevant to AC-05a/AC-05b (pre-embedding properties). Surfaced 3 paths: (A) wait 25 min for live run, (B) static probe (chunk→skip code path without embedding), (C) trust dual-AC vitest fixture as authoritative. Recommended (B). Standing by for ruling.

Ruling: (B) static probe. AC-05a/b are pre-embedding properties; running Ollama for 25 min adds no information about either identity. Dual-AC vitest fixture (affff3a) is authoritative substrate proof; static probe extends to scale proof on real data. Caveat: pin probe script + output in WO directory so future reviewers can replay.

WO-RAG-INDEX-SINGLE-ANALYZER-SLICE-001 complete (closeout-ready). Static probe verify-dual-ac.mjs in WO dir reports AC-05a 2415===2415, AC-05b 263===263, distinctness 2415≠263, graph⇄validation cross-check 263===263. AC-01..AC-11 PASS. AC-12 DIVERGED (+12 net LOC vs estimated negative — AC-03/AC-04 safeguards add ~52 LOC the plan estimate didn't account for; substrate goal achieved). AC-13 deferred to SKILLS-side /close-workorder. 9 commits 9d84677..(closeout) ready to push. STUB-RAG-INDEX-ANALYZER-SLICE-COVERAGE-001 closes via supersession at /close-workorder. Standing by for ORCHESTRATOR to dispatch SKILLS for cross-project /close-workorder.

decisions_log.rationale: After CORE flagged the substrate-level fix post-Option-1-ruling, ORCHESTRATOR pivoted to Option (a) (single analyzer slice). The superseded WO's plan was tactical (expand pattern); this WO is structural (read graph.json directly, delete second analyze call). Replacing the WO rather than amending preserves audit trail for the pivot.

decisions_log.ruling: AC-09 measured as identity (chunksSkipped === header_missing_count exactly), not ±10% band
decisions_log.rationale: DR-SINGLE-SLICE-B (initial). The ±10% band was a probabilistic concession to two-slice divergence. With one slice, identity is the honest target.
decisions_log.superseded_reason: Original wording assumed file-grain comparison (262===262) but graph.json is element-grain. CORE Phase 1 probe surfaced the mismatch before implementation. Ruling refined to dual-AC framing.

decisions_log.ruling: Path (B) — split AC-05 into AC-05a (element-grain identity) + AC-05b (file-grain identity computed via test-code uniqueFiles aggregation). Both load-bearing strict identities, no ±10% tolerance, zero IndexingResult schema delta.
decisions_log.rationale: (A) severs validation-report cross-component contract from Phases 0-8 (same failure mode as yesterday's Option 2). (C) sacrifices RAG retrieval quality for AC wording. (B) preserves both invariants: element-grain captures 'chunks are nodes' substrate truth; file-grain preserves canonical-baseline cross-check. Test-code aggregation operationalizes bulletproof concept #2 (executable invariants) without re-introducing a runtime aggregation seam. Net negative LOC holds (delete ~100, add ~30 src + ~5 test). CORE Phase 1 catch was sharper than the WO author's premise — the grain mismatch was hidden behind 'one graph, one identity' framing that was half-right.

decisions_log.ruling: Path (B) — static probe (chunk→skip code path without embedding) replaces full live rag-index for Phase 4 dynamic verification.
decisions_log.rationale: AC-05a/AC-05b are pre-embedding properties. Live Ollama run adds no information about either identity (it tests AC-08/AC-09/AC-11, all already verified). Dual-AC vitest fixture (affff3a) is authoritative substrate proof; static probe extends to scale proof on real coderef-core data. Verification cost matched to verification target. Caveat: pin probe script + captured output in WO directory.

decisions_log.ruling: AC-12 (net negative LOC in src/integration/rag/*.ts) reported as DIVERGED rather than retro-shimmed.
decisions_log.rationale: Plan estimated 'delete ~100, add ~30'; actual diff is 138/126 = +12 net. The +12 is structurally accountable to AC-03 missing-graph error (~14 LOC) + AC-04 stale-graph fail-loud check (~38 LOC) — both load-bearing safeguards per DR-SINGLE-SLICE-D and ORCHESTRATOR's explicit ask, both with paired tests. The substrate goal (single analyzer slice, AC-05a/b strict identity) IS achieved; LOC count is a poor proxy for substrate complexity here. Reporting truthfully so the closeout artifact and SKILLS' tracking ledger see the actual number rather than a massaged claim.
